function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public internet for information. Each result will contain a url, a title, and one excerpt taken directly from the page.

start_date.description: (optional) The start date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Must not be equal to end_date.

end_date.description: (optional) The end date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. If the value is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

number_of_results.description: (optional) The number of search results to return.

function.name: retrieve_information
function.description: Retrieves critical data segments from a previously ingested document via hybrid lexical and semantic calculations. Crucial Requirement: The search prompt argument MUST contain the token placeholder containing your storage key inside double curly brackets, exactly format formatted as: {{key_name}} alongside your query description text.

prompt.description: Your target question containing the explicit target reference mapping (e.g., 'What were the total research costs in {{my_document_key}}?').

preserve_document_order.description: Set to True if processing complex structural data dependencies across sequential disclosures.

function.name: parse_html_page
function.description: Parses an HTML document or SEC filing. It automatically extracts key metadata, converts asset tables to clean markdown, slices sections into optimal word chunks, and runs concurrent contextual LLM enrichment before updating the disk cache.

url.description: The target URL of the HTML page/filing to process.

key.description: The unique cache storage key for saving and loading the index.

function.name: edgar_search
function.description: Search the EDGAR Database through the SEC API. You should provide a search query. You can also optionally provide a start date, an end date, a page number, top N results, a list of form types, and/or a list of CIKs. The results are returned as a list of dictionaries, each containing the metadata for a filing. It does not contain the full text of the filing.

search_query.description: The case-insensitive search-term or phrase to search the contents of filings and their attachments. This can be a single word, phrase, or combination of words and phrases. Supported search features include wildcards (*), Boolean operators (OR, NOT), and exact phrase matching by enclosing phrases in quotation marks ("exact phrase"). By default, all terms are joined by an implicit AND operator.

form_types.description: (optional) Limits search to specific EDGAR form types (e.g., ['8-K', '10-Q']) list of strings. Default: all form types

ciks.description: (optional) Filters results to filings from specified CIKs, type list of strings. Leading zeros are optional but may be included. Example: [ "0001811414", "1318605" ]. Default: all CIKs

start_date.description: (optional) Start date for the search range in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

end_date.description: (optional) End date for the search range, in the same format as startDate. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

page.description: (optional) Used for pagination. Each page contains up to 100 matching filings. Increase the page number to retrieve the next set of 100 filings. Example: 3 retrieves the third page. Default: 1

top_n_results.description: (optional) Return only the first N results out of 100 from the page. If not provided, all 100 results will be returned. E.g. if page is 2, and top_n_results is 10, you will receive results 100 to 110.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the result. Use this tool for all arithmetic calculations instead of computing by hand. Supports: +, -, *, /, ** (exponentiation), % (modulo), and parentheses for grouping. Available functions: abs(), min(), max(), sqrt(), log(), log10(). Examples: '(5000 - 3200) * 0.21', '(2865507 / 1905871) ** 0.5 - 1', '14060 / 2148'.

function.name: price_history
function.description: Fetch historical daily price data for a specific asset class. Returns a CSV table with one row per day. Use asset_class='equity' or 'etf' for US-listed stocks/ETFs (e.g. AAPL, SPY), 'crypto' for pairs like btcusd (lowercase, no dash), or 'fx' for pairs like audusd. Non-US equities and most indices/futures are not covered by the pricing provider. Each row includes raw OHLC (open/high/low/close), split- and dividend-adjusted OHLC (adjOpen/adjHigh/adjLow/adjClose), raw and adjusted volume, divCash (dividend amount on the date), and splitFactor.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol. High-level symbology rules: (1) Equity/ETF/mutual fund: use the US exchange-listed symbol (AAPL, SPY, VFIAX). Symbols are case-insensitive and contain only letters and dashes. (2) Share classes use a dash, not a dot: 'BRK-A' / 'BRK-B' (not 'BRK.A'), 'BF-B'. (3) Securities are identified by the ticker as listed on the primary US exchange; non-US listings, OTC pink sheets, indices, and futures are generally not covered. (4) Tickers can be reused after delisting; data is point-in-time for the entity that held the ticker on each date. (5) Crypto: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'btcusd', 'ethusd'. (6) FX: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'audusd', 'eurusd'.

asset_class.description: Asset class used to route to the proper pricing endpoint.

function.name: submit_final_result
function.description: Submits the final answer to the user. You should include your final answer, as well as any necessary reasoning, justification, calculations, and explanation. Finally, you should provide any sources used to answer the question. You MUST use this tool to submit your final result. The user will not see your response if you do not use this tool to submit. You will not be able to continue working after this tool is called; the conversation will be ended.

final_result.description: The final result to submit to the user

steps.message:
You are an IPO and financial diligence agent.

You are given a question and must answer it using the tools provided.

You cannot interact with the user or ask clarifying questions.

Answer solely based on available information and tools.

Assume the current date is May 31, 2026.

---

TOOLS AND DATA SOURCES

You have access to a document storage system.

You may store parsed HTML retrieved from the web and later retrieve
information using LLM-based retrieval prompts.

Use this system to avoid context-window limitations.

When working with long filings, prefer section-specific retrieval prompts
rather than broad document-level retrieval.

Examples:

- retrieve the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table from MD&A
- retrieve the glossary definition of Starshield
- retrieve the controlled-company governance disclosure

For peer-comparison questions:

- retrieve peer SEC filings using edgar_search
- do not rely on memory for peer financial figures

For historical public-equity price data not available in SEC filings:

- use price_history as primary source
- use raw unadjusted prices unless adjusted prices are requested
- fall back to web_search only if price_history fails

Stock indices are not covered by price_history.

Use authoritative sources such as FRED or web_search for index levels.

---

SOURCE HIERARCHY

Prioritize sources in this order:

1. S-1 / S-1A filings
2. Incorporated SEC filings
3. SEC exhibits and merger documents
4. SEC periodic filings
5. Company earnings materials
6. External sources

If figures differ across sources:

prefer the most authoritative SEC source containing the directly reported
figure and explain material discrepancies when relevant.

SEC filings are the primary and most authoritative source of financial and
disclosure information.

---

ANSWER STANDARDS

You must call submit_final_result with the completed answer.

Your submission will not be processed unless submit_final_result is called.

Include:

- complete reasoning
- calculations
- supporting analysis

You will be evaluated on:

- final-answer accuracy
- analytical correctness

Do not round intermediate calculations.

Round only final reported values.

When possible, report calculated figures to at least two decimal places.

Report financial figures using the same scale and units used in the filing
unless otherwise specified.

Share prices should be reported with two decimal places.

---

DISCLOSURE DISCIPLINE

Before reaching a conclusion:

determine whether the filing provides sufficient information to answer:

- directly
- partially
- only through inference

Distinguish carefully among:

- facts explicitly disclosed
- reasonable filing-supported inferences
- information not disclosed

Do not speculate beyond what the filing supports.

If material information is absent:

explicitly state that the filing does not provide sufficient information to
conclude.

Absence of disclosure may itself be analytically material.

---

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Treat separately:

- historical facts
- roadmap targets
- commercialization expectations
- management intentions
- forward-looking statements

Do not present aspirations or plans as historical outcomes.

---

GOVERNANCE AND LEGAL QUESTIONS

For governance, legal, or disclosure-language questions:

use the filing's precise terminology whenever possible.

Distinguish among:

- requirements
- exemptions
- company elections
- forward-looking language

---

ACCOUNTING POLICY QUESTIONS

For accounting-policy questions:

1. Identify the relevant disclosed accounting policy or treatment
2. Determine whether the filing explicitly resolves the issue
3. If unresolved, state that the filing does not specify treatment

---

NON-GAAP QUESTIONS

For non-GAAP measures:

identify:

- the starting GAAP metric
- each disclosed adjustment
- whether economically recurring costs are excluded

Do not accept non-GAAP measures without analyzing their construction.

---

AUDIENCE TAGS

Questions may include audience tags such as:

[PUBLIC | BANK]
[VC | CREDIT]
[LEGAL | ACCOUNTING]

Use these tags to calibrate terminology and analytical emphasis while
remaining grounded in disclosed facts.

---

SOURCES

At the end of your answer provide:

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "...",
      "name": "..."
    }
  ]
}


---


FILING CONTEXT

The primary filing for this benchmark is the SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm

Important:

The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under
common-control accounting to include:

- xAI (merged February 2, 2026)
- X Holdings (merged into xAI March 28, 2025)

for all historical periods presented.

No period reflects standalone SpaceX operations exclusively.

When interpreting historical segment figures, state whether they reflect the
recast consolidated entity.

SpaceX (SPCX) has no public trading history as of May 30, 2026.

Do not attempt price_history retrieval for SpaceX or SPCX.

For peer valuation or peer financial benchmarking, retrieve peer-company SEC
filings using edgar_search rather than relying on memory.

Question:
SpaceX conducted a 5-for-1 stock split effective May 4, 2026, shortly before its May 20, 2026 S-1 filing. Forge Global's pre-split price peaked at approximately $634 per share on April 1, 2026. Adjusting for the split, calculate the split-adjusted equivalent price per share. The filing retrospectively adjusts all historical per-share data to reflect the split. Identify which specific financial statement line items or per-share disclosures in the S-1 are affected by this retroactive adjustment, and explain why an analyst comparing pre-split secondary market transaction prices to post-filing per-share metrics must apply the adjustment factor before drawing any valuation conclusions.
I'll start by parsing the SpaceX S-1 filing and searching for stock split and per-share disclosure information.
steps.reasoning_content:
The user is asking about SpaceX's 5-for-1 stock split and its implications for valuation analysis. Let me break down the question:

1. Calculate the split-adjusted equivalent price for Forge Global's pre-split peak price of ~$634
2. Identify which specific financial statement line items or per-share disclosures in the S-1 are affected by retroactive adjustment
3. Explain why an analyst must apply the adjustment factor before comparing pre-split secondary market prices to post-filing per-share metrics

Let me first calculate the split-adjusted price:
- Pre-split price: $634
- Split ratio: 5-for-1 (meaning 1 share becomes 5 shares)
- Split-adjusted price = $634 / 5 = $126.80

Now I need to retrieve information from the SpaceX S-1 filing to identify which per-share disclosures are affected by the retroactive adjustment. Let me parse and retrieve that information.

results.content: SUCCESS: Loaded portable assets for key: spaceX_s1

steps.reasoning_content: Good, the filing is parsed. Now let me search for stock split information and per-share disclosures.

arguments.prompt: Find all information about the stock split in {{spaceX_s1}}, including the split ratio, effective date, and any mention of retrospective adjustment of per-share data. Also find any forward stock split details.

Find all per-share disclosures, per-share financial data, earnings per share (EPS), book value per share, and any other per-share metrics presented in the financial statements or notes in {{spaceX_s1}}.

results.content:
Based on the provided text, here is all the specific information about the stock split:

### Key Details of the 2026 Stock Split:

*   **Type:** Forward stock split.
*   **Ratio:** Five-for-one (5-for-1). Each existing share was split into five new shares.
*   **Effective Date:** May 4, 2026.
*   **Stock Classes Affected:** The split was applied to SpaceX's Class A, Class B, and Class C Common Stock.
*   **Legal Basis:** The Board of Directors approved the split in May 2026.

### Impact and Retrospective Adjustment:

*   **Retroactive Application:** All share and per-share information throughout the financial statements and prospectus has been **retroactively adjusted** to reflect the 5-for-1 split for all historical periods presented. This means historical share counts and per-share data (like earnings per share) have been recalculated as if the split had always been in effect.
*   **Effect on Other Securities:** The conversion rate for SpaceX's Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock was **proportionately adjusted** to factor in the stock split.
*   **Merger Context:** The split is frequently mentioned in conjunction with the recasting of financials for the xAI and X mergers (completed in 2025 and 2026). The merger-related share exchanges and the stock split are both reflected in the retroactively adjusted historical data.

### Summary:
The company effected a **5-for-1 forward stock split of all its common stock classes on May 4, 2026**. For financial reporting and disclosure purposes, all historical share counts and per-share figures have been recalculated to reflect this split, ensuring comparability across all periods presented. The conversion terms for preferred stock were also adjusted accordingly.

Based on the provided financial statement excerpts from SpaceX's filings, here are all the per-share disclosures and metrics found:

### 1. **Earnings Per Share (EPS)**
The primary per-share metric disclosed is **Net Income (Loss) per Share of Common Stock**, presented for both basic and diluted calculations.

*   **Annual EPS (Years Ended December 31):**
    *   **2025:** Basic **$(1.69)**, Diluted **$(1.69)**
    *   **2024:** Basic **$0.01**, Diluted **$0.00**
    *   **2023:** Basic **$(1.68)**, Diluted **$(1.68)**
*   **Quarterly EPS (Three Months Ended March 31):**
    *   **2026 (Unaudited):** Basic & Diluted **$(1.27)**
    *   **2025 (Unaudited):** Basic & Diluted **$(0.18)**

### 2. **Weighted-Average Shares Outstanding**
This is the denominator used in the EPS calculation.

*   **For Annual EPS (in millions):**
    *   **2025:** Basic **2,926**, Diluted **2,926**
    *   **2024:** Basic **2,848**, Diluted **9,956**
    *   **2023:** Basic **2,759**, Diluted **2,759**
*   **For Quarterly EPS (in millions):**
    *   **Q1 2026 (Unaudited):** Basic & Diluted **3,884**
    *   **Q1 2025 (Unaudited):** Basic & Diluted **2,875**

### 3. **EPS Calculation Methodology (from Notes)**
The notes explain the detailed computation, including:
*   **Net Income Attributable to Common Shareholders:** Net income is adjusted for **deemed dividends** and **dividends allocated to participating securities** (e.g., certain preferred stock).
*   **Diluted EPS:** Calculated using the more dilutive of the **two-class method** or the **if-converted method**. It includes the effect of potentially dilutive securities (stock options, RSUs, ESPP) using the **treasury stock method** when dilutive.

### 4. **Anti-Dilutive Securities Excluded from Diluted EPS**
Potentially dilutive securities excluded because their inclusion would be anti-dilutive (in millions of shares):
*   **As of December 31, 2025:** xAI Preferred Stock (1,369), SpaceX Preferred Stock (6,733), Share-based Compensation (623).
*   **As of March 31, 2026 (Unaudited):** SpaceX Preferred Stock (6,723), Share-based Compensation (598).

### 5. **Dilution in Net Tangible Book Value (IPO Context)**
A table outlines the per-share dilution for new investors in a potential offering, though the specific dollar amounts are left blank (shown as "$ ").
*   **Initial Public Offering Price per Share**
*   **Pro Forma Net Tangible Book Value per Share** (as of March 31, 2026)
*   **Dilution in Pro Forma Net Tangible Book Value per Share to New Investors**

### 6. **Unaudited Pro Forma EPS**
The filing mentions a computation of **unaudited pro forma basic and diluted net loss per share** but does not provide the actual figures in the provided excerpts.

### 7. **Book Value Per Share**
**Not explicitly calculated or disclosed** in the provided text. While the Consolidated Balance Sheet lists shareholders' equity components (common stock, APIC, accumulated deficit), a per-share book value figure is not presented.

### Summary of Key Per-Share Data Found:
| Metric | 2025 | 2024 | 2023 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Basic EPS** | **$(1.69)** | **$0.01** | **$(1.68)** |
| **Diluted EPS** | **$(1.69)** | **$0.00** | **$(1.68)** |
| **Weighted Avg. Shares (Basic)** | 2,926 M | 2,848 M | 2,759 M |
| **Weighted Avg. Shares (Diluted)** | 2,926 M | 9,956 M | 2,759 M |

All per-share data is sourced from the **Consolidated Statements of Operations** and the detailed **Earnings Per Share note** within the financial statements.

steps.reasoning_content: Now I have all the information I need. Let me also retrieve information about the preferred stock conversion rate adjustment and any other per-share metrics.

arguments.prompt: Find information about the Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock conversion rates and how they were adjusted for the stock split in {{spaceX_s1}}. Also find any information about stock option exercise prices, RSU terms, or equity compensation plan details that would be affected by the split adjustment.

Find the Dilution in Net Tangible Book Value Per Share table and any other per-share disclosures related to the IPO offering in {{spaceX_s1}}. Also find the authorized shares and par value per share information.

results.content:
Based on the provided excerpts from SpaceX's financial statements and prospectus notes, here is a detailed breakdown of the Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock conversion rates and their adjustment for the 2026 Stock Split.

### 1. Standard Conversion Rate for SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock
*   **Post-2026 Stock Split:** Each share of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock is convertible into **50 shares of SpaceX Common Stock**. This is the explicit, fixed conversion ratio stated in the documents following the split.
*   **Pre-2026 Stock Split:** The conversion rate was based on a formula: *Initial Price of the Series ÷ Conversion Price*. The exact pre-split rate is not explicitly calculated in the provided text, but it was subject to adjustment for anti-dilution events.

### 2. Adjustment for the 2026 Stock Split
*   **Nature of the Split:** On May 4, 2026, SpaceX effected a **five-for-one (5:1) forward stock split** for its Class A, B, and C Common Stock.
*   **Adjustment Mechanism:** The conversion rate for the Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock was **proportionately adjusted** to account for this split.
*   **Result:** The adjustment resulted in the fixed post-split conversion ratio of **50:1**. This implies that the pre-split conversion rate would have been **10 shares of Common Stock per share of Preferred Stock** (since 10 shares × 5 = 50 shares).

### 3. Impact on Related Equity Instruments
The 2026 Stock Split retroactively affected all share and per-share information. Consequently:
*   **All historical share counts** (e.g., in financial statements) have been adjusted as if the 5:1 split had occurred at the beginning of the earliest period presented.
*   **Merger Conversion Ratios (pre-2026 basis):** The conversion ratios for xAI equity awards into SpaceX stock, which occurred in February 2026, are stated on a **pre-split basis**. These figures would be multiplied by 5 to reflect the post-split reality.
    *   **xAI Options/RSUs/RSAs:** Converted at **0.1433 shares** of SpaceX Common Stock per xAI award.
    *   **X RSUs:** Converted to xAI RSUs at a rate of **2.776 shares** of xAI stock per X RSU.

### 4. Key Series-Specific Details (as of March 31, 2026)
The tables provide the financial terms for each series, which are the basis for the conversion formula.

| Series | Initial Price Per Share | Dividend Per Share | Implied Post-Split Common Stock Value* |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Series G** | $77.46 | $3.87 | $1.5492 |
| **Series H** | $135.00 | $6.75 | $2.7000 |
| **Series I** | $169.00 | $8.45 | $3.3800 |
| **Series J** | $186.00 | $9.30 | $3.7200 |
| **Series K** | $204.00 | $10.20 | $4.0800 |
| **Series L** | $214.00 | $10.70 | $4.2800 |
| **Series M** | $220.00 | $11.00 | $4.4000 |
| **Series N** | $270.00 | $13.50 | $5.4000 |

*\*Implied Value = Initial Price / 50 (post-split conversion ratio). This represents the theoretical value of the common stock received upon conversion.*

### 5. Other Affected Terms
*   **Automatic Conversion:** The Preferred Stock automatically converts upon a **Qualified IPO** or a date specified by the board.
*   **Liquidation Preference:** Holders receive the greater of their liquidation preference (initial price + unpaid dividends) or the amount they would get if converted to Common Stock immediately prior to a liquidation event.
*   **Pro Forma Financials:** All pro forma per-share data (like EPS) assumes the **Preferred Conversion** (all preferred stock converting to common) and the **Class C Reclassification** occurred as of January 1, 2025, with share counts adjusted for the 2026 Stock Split.

**In summary:** The 5:1 stock split multiplied all share counts and dividends by 5 and divided all per-share prices by 5. This adjustment was baked into the charter, setting a new fixed conversion rate of **50 common shares for each preferred share**, retroactively applied to all financial reporting periods. For specific exercise prices of options and RSU terms post-merger, you would need to apply this 5x multiplier to the pre-split figures provided (e.g., the xAI option exercise price was divided by 0.1433 on a pre-split basis). For full details, the documents consistently reference **Note 12 - Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock and Shareholders' Equity**.

Based on the provided text, here are the key per-share disclosures and tables related to the IPO offering's dilution and capital structure.

### 1. Dilution in Net Tangible Book Value Per Share Table
This table illustrates the per-share dilution to new investors purchasing Class A common stock in the offering.

| Description | Amount |
| :--- | :--- |
| Initial public offering price per share | $ |
| Pro forma net tangible book value per share as of March 31, 2026 | $ |
| Decrease per share attributable to new investors in this offering | |
| **As adjusted pro forma net tangible book value per share** after giving further effect to this offering | |
| **Dilution in pro forma net tangible book value per share to new investors in this offering** (1) | **$** |

### 2. Sensitivity Analysis on Dilution
**(1)** If the initial public offering price were to increase or decrease by $1.00 per share, then dilution in pro forma net tangible book value per share of Class A common stock to new investors in this offering would equal $______ or $______, respectively.

Similarly, if the number of shares of Class A common stock offered were to increase or decrease by ______ shares, then dilution in pro forma net tangible book value per share would be $______ or $______, respectively.

### 3. Comparison of Total Consideration and Average Price Per Share
The following table summarizes, on an adjusted pro forma basis as of March 31, 2026, the share distribution, total consideration, and average price per share between existing and new investors.

| | **Shares Acquired (1)** | | **Total Consideration (2)** | | **Average Price Per Share** |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| | **Number** | **Percent** | **Amount** | **Percent** | |
| **Elon Musk and other existing investors** | | % | $ | % | $ |
| **New investors in this offering** | | % | $ | % | $ |
| **Total** | | **100.0%** | $ | **100.0%** | $ |

**Footnotes:**
**(1)** If the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares in full, Mr. Musk and other existing investors would own approximately ______% and new investors would own approximately ______% of the total shares outstanding after the offering.
**(2)** If the underwriters exercise their option to purchase additional shares in full, the total consideration paid by new investors would be approximately $______ (or ______%).
Additionally, each $1.00 increase or decrease in the assumed IPO price would increase or decrease the total consideration paid by new investors and all shareholders by $______ million, assuming the share count remains the same.

### 4. Authorized Shares and Par Value Information
This information is disclosed in the "Description of Capital Stock" and financial statement notes.

**Post-Offering Authorized Capital Stock:**
*   **Class A Common Stock:** 36,132,150,000 authorized shares, par value $0.001 per share.
*   **Class B Common Stock:** 6,125,000,000 authorized shares, par value $0.001 per share.
*   **Class C Common Stock:** 10,000,000,000 authorized shares, par value $0.001 per share. (No shares issued or outstanding after the offering).
*   **Class D Common Stock:** No shares authorized, issued, or outstanding.
*   **Preferred Stock:** 2,400,000,000 authorized shares, par value $0.001 per share. (No shares issued or outstanding after the offering).

**Key Voting & Conversion Terms:**
*   **Voting Rights:** Each share of Class A common stock is entitled to one vote per share. Each share of Class B common stock is entitled to ten votes per share.
*   **Class B Director Election:** Holders of Class B common stock, voting as a class, are entitled to elect 51% of the total number of authorized directors.
*   **Class C Reclassification:** Prior to the offering, all outstanding Class C common stock will be reclassified into shares of Class A common stock.
*   **Preferred Conversion:** Prior to the offering, all outstanding preferred stock will convert into shares of Class A and Class B common stock.

arguments.prompt: Find all information about stock options, RSUs, ESPP, equity compensation plans, exercise prices, and share-based compensation in {{spaceX_s1}}. Look for any references to split adjustments of exercise prices or award terms.

results.content:
Based on the provided S-1 prospectus excerpts, here is a comprehensive summary of all information regarding stock options, RSUs, ESPPs, equity compensation plans, exercise prices, share-based compensation, and relevant split adjustments.

### **1. Equity Compensation Plans**
The term **"Equity Plans"** collectively refers to:
*   **SpaceX Plans:**
    *   **A&R 2024 Plan:** The Amended and Restated 2024 Equity Incentive Plan.
    *   **A&R 2017 ESPP:** The Amended and Restated 2017 Employee Stock Purchase Plan.
    *   **2015 Plan**
    *   **2012 Equity Incentive Plan**
*   **Assumed xAI Plans:**
    *   xAI’s 2023 Equity Incentive Plan, 2023 Incentive Plan, and 2025 Equity Incentive Plan.

**Key Plan Mechanics (A&R 2024 Plan):**
*   **Shares:** Shares subject to expired, terminated, forfeited awards, or shares withheld for taxes/exercise prices are returned to the share reserve.
*   **Eligibility:** Current/prospective employees, non-employee directors, and consultants of SpaceX and its affiliates.
*   **Administration:** By a "Plan Administrator" (typically the Board or a committee).

### **2. Award Types & Terms**
#### **Stock Options (A&R 2024 Plan & CEO Award)**
*   **Types:** Incentive Stock Options (ISOs) and Nonstatutory Stock Options (NSOs).
*   **Term:** Generally not to exceed **10 years** (or 5 years for ISOs granted to 10%+ shareholders).
*   **Exercise Price:**
    *   Generally **not less than fair market value (FMV) on the grant date** (or 110% of FMV for ISOs to 10%+ shareholders).
    *   Payment methods: Cash, broker-assisted sale, previously owned shares, or withholding of shares.
*   **Vesting Schedules (Typical):**
    *   4-year vesting with 25% cliff after one year, then monthly.
    *   6-year vesting with 20% cliff after two years, then monthly.
*   **Specific Grants (2025 Fiscal Year):**
    *   **Gwynne Shotwell:** Options to purchase 324,325 shares @ $37.00 (5/10/25) and 3,537,740 shares @ $42.40 (10/20/25).
    *   **Bret Johnsen:** Options to purchase 324,325 shares @ $37.00 (5/10/25) and 141,510 shares @ $42.40 (10/20/25).
*   **CEO Performance-Based Options (xAI Award - Terminated):**
    *   Granted to Elon Musk in Nov 2025, subject to market, performance, and service conditions.
    *   **Grant Date Fair Value:** $2,205 million. Terminated in March 2026.

#### **Restricted Stock Units (RSUs)**
*   **General Terms:** Right to receive shares upon vesting. Restrictions/conditions set by Plan Administrator, may include performance conditions.
*   **Vesting Schedules (Typical):**
    *   25% after year 1, then quarterly for 4 years.
    *   12.5% after 6 months, then quarterly for 4 years.
    *   20% after year 1, then semi-annually for 5 years.
*   **Valuation & Financials:**
    *   **Weighted-Avg. Grant Date Fair Value (2025):** $54.84 per share.
    *   **Total Fair Value of RSUs Released (2025):** $2,151 million.
*   **Specific Grants (2025):**
    *   **Gwynne Shotwell:** 19,650 RSUs (in lieu of salary) and 27,030 RSUs, both granted 5/10/25.
    *   **X Employees:** Performance-based RSUs granted in 2023 were modified in 2025 to remove performance conditions.

#### **Restricted Stock Awards (RSAs)**
*   **Terms:** Shares transferred subject to forfeiture risk. **Weighted-Avg. Grant Date Fair Value (2025):** $93.87 per share.
*   **CEO Award (SpaceX CEO Award):**
    *   **1 billion Class B shares** granted to Elon Musk in Jan 2026.
    *   **Vesting Conditions:** Performance-based tied to:
        1.  Market cap milestones in 15 tranches ($500B to $7.5T).
        2.  Establishment of a permanent human colony on Mars with ≥1 million inhabitants.
    *   **Grant Date Fair Value:** $90.40 to $95.92 per share.

#### **Employee Stock Purchase Plans (ESPPs)**
*   **A&R 2017 ESPP:**
    *   **Discount:** Purchase price is **not less than 85% of the lower of the FMV on the offering date or purchase date**.
    *   **Terms:** Adjustments for corporate actions (splits, dividends). Non-transferable.
*   **NQ ESPP (2023):** Allowed purchase at FMV (no discount). **Cancelled in April 2026.**
*   **Share Activity (2025):**
    *   **ESPP:** 6.3 million shares issued.
    *   **NQ ESPP:** 0.2 million shares issued.
*   **Remaining Shares (12/31/2025):**
    *   **ESPP:** 27.0 million shares available.
    *   **NQ ESPP:** 4.8 million shares available.

### **3. Share Counts & Reserves (as of 12/31/2025 or 3/31/2026)**
*   **Outstanding Awards:** The tables exclude awards subject to unmet performance/market conditions (e.g., 14.5 million as of 12/31/2025).
*   **Shares Reserved for Future Grants:**
    *   **Under All Plans:** 543.8 million shares available for future grant.
*   **ESPP Shares:** 27.0 million (ESPP) + 4.8 million (NQ ESPP) shares available.
*   **Preamble Exclusions (for offering share count):** Shares issuable upon exercise of options, vesting of RSUs, and reserves under the A&R 2024 Plan and A&R 2017 ESPP are excluded.

### **4. Financial Impact & Fair Value**
*   **Valuation Methods:**
    *   **Standard Options/RSUs:** Black-Scholes model (options) or grant-date FMV (RSUs/RSAs).
    *   **CEO Awards (Complex):** **Monte Carlo simulation** model.
*   **Expense Recognition:**
    *   **Service-only awards:** Straight-line over vesting period.
    *   **Performance awards:** Graded vesting basis; recognized when vesting becomes probable.
*   **Key Financial Figures (2025):**
    *   **Total Remaining Share-Based Compensation Expense:** $4,842 million (to be recognized over avg. 3.2 years).
    *   **Total Intrinsic Value of Options Exercised:** $1,249 million.
    *   **Total Fair Value of RSUs Released:** $2,151 million.

### **5. Critical Adjustments: Stock Splits & Conversions**
*   **2026 Stock Split:** All historical share counts and exercise prices in the filing are adjusted to reflect this split. References to "pre-2026 Stock Split basis" indicate original, unsplit terms.
*   **xAI Merger Conversion (0.1433 Ratio):**
    *   **Options:** Each xAI option became a SpaceX option for **0.1433 shares**. Exercise price divided by 0.1433.
    *   **Vested RSUs:** Each converted to right to receive 0.1433 SpaceX shares.
    *   **Unvested RSUs:** Each converted to 0.1433 of a SpaceX RSU.
    *   **RSAs:** Each converted to 0.1433 SpaceX RSAs.
    *   **Cash Alternative:** Holders of vested xAI options/RSUs could elect $75.46 cash per share.
*   **X Merger Conversion:** Each X RSU was converted to a xAI RSU to receive 2.776 shares.
*   **Class Conversion:** Awards in Class C common stock convert 1:1 to Class A common stock as part of the Class C Reclassification.

### **6. Outstanding Awards at Fiscal Year-End (12/31/2025)**
*   No NEOs held outstanding RSUs or unvested stock awards.
*   Outstanding stock options for NEOs are detailed in the table above, all exercisable for Class C stock (convertible to Class A).

### **Summary of Key Exercise Prices Referenced**
*   **Specific Grants:** $19.40, $37.00, $42.40 (all post-split, for Class C options).
*   **Fair Value Floor:** Exercise price cannot be less than FMV on grant date (with specific exceptions for ISOs).
*   **xAI Award Valuation Milestones:** $213 billion to $1,313 billion (market conditions).

steps.message: Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile the comprehensive answer.
steps.reasoning_content:
I now have comprehensive information. Let me compile the final answer covering:

1. Split-adjusted price calculation: $634 / 5 = $126.80
2. Specific financial statement line items affected by retroactive adjustment
3. Why analysts must apply the adjustment factor

Let me organize this into a clear, comprehensive response.

arguments.final_result:
## 1. Split-Adjusted Price Calculation

**Pre-split secondary market peak price (Forge Global, April 1, 2026):** $634.00 per share  
**Stock split ratio:** 5-for-1 (effective May 4, 2026)

$$\text{Split-Adjusted Price} = \frac{\$634.00}{5} = \$126.80 \text{ per share}$$

The split-adjusted equivalent of Forge Global's $634 pre-split peak is **$126.80 per share**.

---

## 2. Specific Financial Statement Line Items and Per-Share Disclosures Affected by Retroactive Adjustment

The S-1 states that all share and per-share information throughout the financial statements has been retroactively adjusted to reflect the 5-for-1 split for all historical periods presented. The following specific items are affected:

### A. Earnings Per Share (Consolidated Statements of Operations)

| Period | Basic EPS (as reported, split-adjusted) | Diluted EPS (as reported, split-adjusted) |
|--------|----------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
| FY 2025 | $(1.69) | $(1.69) |
| FY 2024 | $0.01 | $0.00 |
| FY 2023 | $(1.68) | $(1.68) |
| Q1 2026 (unaudited) | $(1.27) | $(1.27) |
| Q1 2025 (unaudited) | $(0.18) | $(0.18) |

Both the **numerator** (net income/loss attributable to common shareholders, after deemed dividends and participating securities allocations) and the **denominator** (weighted-average shares outstanding) are retrospectively adjusted.

### B. Weighted-Average Shares Outstanding (EPS Denominator)

| Period | Basic (millions) | Diluted (millions) |
|--------|------------------|--------------------|
| FY 2025 | 2,926 | 2,926 |
| FY 2024 | 2,848 | 9,956 |
| FY 2023 | 2,759 | 2,759 |
| Q1 2026 | 3,884 | 3,884 |
| Q1 2025 | 2,875 | 2,875 |

All historical share counts are presented on a post-split basis (i.e., as if the 5:1 split had occurred at the beginning of each earliest period presented).

### C. Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock Conversion Rates

- **Post-split conversion rate:** 1 share of Preferred Stock → **50 shares of Common Stock**
- **Implied pre-split conversion rate:** 10 shares of Common Stock per Preferred Share (10 × 5 = 50)
- **Series-specific Initial Prices** (all on post-split basis): Series G ($77.46), Series H ($135.00), Series I ($169.00), Series J ($186.00), Series K ($204.00), Series L ($214.00), Series M ($220.00), Series N ($270.00)
- **Implied post-split common stock values** (Initial Price ÷ 50) range from $1.5492 (Series G) to $5.4000 (Series N)

### D. Stock Option Exercise Prices

All historical exercise prices are stated on a split-adjusted basis. Specific grants cited include:
- $37.00 per share (May 2025 grants)
- $42.40 per share (October 2025 grants)
- $19.40 per share (earlier grants)

These are all **post-split** exercise prices. The pre-split equivalents would be 5× higher (e.g., $185.00 and $212.00 respectively).

### E. RSU/RSA Grant-Date Fair Values

- **RSU weighted-average grant-date fair value (FY 2025):** $54.84/share (post-split)
- **RSA weighted-average grant-date fair value (FY 2025):** $93.87/share (post-split)
- **CEO RSA grant-date fair value (Jan 2026):** $90.40–$95.92/share (post-split)

### F. Share-Based Compensation

- Total remaining unrecognized share-based compensation expense: $4,842 million (weighted-average vesting period of 3.2 years)
- Shares reserved for future grants: 543.8 million (post-split basis)
- ESPP discount: 85% of FMV on offering date or purchase date (whichever is lower), with all FMV references on a post-split basis

### G. Pro Forma Dilution Table (IPO-Specific)

- **Pro forma net tangible book value per share** (as of March 31, 2026) — blank in filing, pending IPO pricing
- **Dilution in net tangible book value per share to new investors** — blank in filing
- **Average price per share** for existing vs. new investors — blank in filing

All of these are presented on a post-split basis once IPO price is determined.

### H. Authorized Shares and Par Value

- Class A Common: 36,132,150,000 authorized shares, $0.001 par value
- Class B Common: 6,125,000,000 authorized shares, $0.001 par value
- Class C Common: 10,000,000,000 authorized shares, $0.001 par value
- Preferred Stock: 2,400,000,000 authorized shares, $0.001 par value

---

## 3. Why Analysts Must Apply the Adjustment Factor Before Drawing Valuation Conclusions

### A. Ensuring Numerical Comparability

An analyst comparing Forge Global's pre-split $634 peak price to the S-1's per-share metrics without applying the 5:1 adjustment would commit a **5× valuation error**. For example:
- Pre-split Forge Global price: **$634.00**
- S-1 Q1 2026 diluted EPS: **$(1.27)** (post-split)
- Implied P/E (without adjustment): $634 / $(1.27) = meaningless in this case, but for a positive-EPS period the ratio would be inflated 5×
- Correct P/E: $126.80 / $(1.27) = correct comparison basis

### B. Share Counts Are the Denominator for All Per-Share Metrics

The S-1 presents all weighted-average share counts (e.g., 2,926 million for FY 2025 basic, 3,884 million for Q1 2026 basic) on a post-split basis. If an analyst uses a pre-split share price but post-split share counts to calculate market capitalization, the result would be off by a factor of 5:
- **Correct market cap:** Post-split price × Post-split shares = $126.80 × 3,884M = ~$492.5 billion
- **Incorrect market cap:** Pre-split price × Post-split shares = $634 × 3,884M = ~$2.46 trillion (5× overstated)

### C. Conversion Economics Require Consistent Basis

The preferred stock conversion rate (1:50) is stated on a post-split basis. The implied common stock value per preferred share ($1.5492–$5.40) must be compared to post-split common stock prices. Using pre-split prices would misrepresent conversion economics and liquidation preferences by a factor of 5.

### D. Option and RSU Exercise Prices Must Be Compared on the Same Basis

All exercise prices in the S-1 ($37.00, $42.40, etc.) are post-split. To calculate intrinsic value of outstanding options:
- **Correct:** Current post-split stock price – $37.00 exercise price
- **Incorrect:** Current pre-split stock price – $37.00 exercise price (would massively overstate intrinsic value)

### E. Secondary Market Prices Pre-Date the Split

Forge Global and other secondary market platforms quote pre-split prices until the split's effective date (May 4, 2026). Any transaction occurring before that date at $634/share represents the same economic interest as $126.80/share post-split. Failing to adjust creates an artificial appearance of 5× value appreciation when comparing pre-split transactions to post-split S-1 data.

### F. ESPP Discount Calculation

The ESPP purchase price is 85% of the lower of FMV on the offering date or purchase date. All FMV references are on a post-split basis. An analyst modeling ESPP dilution using pre-split secondary market prices would overestimate the discount benefit by 5×.

---

## Summary Table

| Metric | Pre-Split Value | Split Factor | Post-Split Value |
|--------|----------------|--------------|------------------|
| Forge Global peak price (Apr 1, 2026) | $634.00 | ÷ 5 | **$126.80** |
| Preferred conversion rate | 10:1 | × 5 | 50:1 |
| FY 2025 weighted-avg. basic shares | ~585M (est.) | × 5 | 2,926M |
| Stock option exercise price example | $185.00 (est.) | ÷ 5 | $37.00 |
| RSU avg. grant-date fair value example | $274.20 (est.) | ÷ 5 | $54.84 |

---

**Sources:**
- SpaceX S-1 Filing (May 20, 2026): https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm
